sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_files (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, file_path VARCHAR(255), size INTEGER);
SELECT parse_filename(file_path, true, 'forward_slash') AS filename, size * 2 AS double_size FROM test_files;
SELECT id, parse_filename(file_path, true, 'both_slash') AS filename, (size * NULL) AS size_null_handling FROM test_files WHERE size IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, parse_filename(file_path, true, 'backslash') AS filename, size * 1000 AS big_size FROM test_files WHERE size < 150;
SELECT id, parse_filename(file_path, true, 'system') AS base_filename, size FROM test_files WHERE size = (SELECT MAX(size) FROM test_files);

WITH size_calculated AS (SELECT id, parse_filename(file_path, false, 'system') AS filename, size * 10 AS adjusted_size FROM test_files) SELECT filename, adjusted_size FROM size_calculated WHERE adjusted_size > 500;
CREATE TABLE test_join (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, category VARCHAR(100));
SELECT tf.id, parse_filename(tf.file_path, true, 'system') AS filename, (tf.size * tj.id) AS product FROM test_files tf JOIN test_join tj ON size < tj.id * 100;
SELECT parse_filename(file_path, true, 'both_slash') AS filename, SUM(size) AS total_size FROM test_files GROUP BY filename HAVING total_size < 300;
